generatedSentence: Kael, a scruffy hacker and underdog, had a vast backlog of cases. He lived roadside near the murky ocean terminus, his only hobbies being gardening a small juniper bush and reading ancient books. He understood the urbane minister's palatial business was built on lossless data theft, granting them immunity. This was a constant warning, a modern fable of power. His nerves were always on edge, knowing his meticulous preparations for entry needed to discover the core theory of their black hole operations, despite the hardship. This tactical mission was temporary, a mere tangent from his boring everyday life, but driven by a deep vengeance. He knew the pickpockets and rowdy guards were just a trick, not the true obstacle. The whole operation was under lockdown. His destiny was clear: to inject a trojan, overclocking their systems past their capacity. He needed to be fast, like an acrobat doing a backflip, avoiding radars and saccade movements. A specialist lockkeeper secured the station, while a veejay kept the guards distracted with tonal frequencies and pictures of nymphets. He had no time for mojitos. The occidental security system was omnipotent, protected by a deejay's alarms, its tectonic plates shifting with every entry attempt. Kael knew it was a guessing game, requiring humility, not temper. He used knifes for precise cuts, avoiding detection. The skyjack and hijacker, part of a diversion, drew attention while he slipped past, handgun holstered. He witnessed a deputy licking a bone, a bizarre sight. Then, the grim outcome: he saw pictures of his family, worshipped by kings, their faces amber from nutrient deficiency, a stark testimony to the cruelty. This wasn't just business; it was personal. With chagrin, he realized his underwear was showing as he performed a quick backflip, narrowly escaping detection. This was a wholly unexpected ramification. He observed the grand regional geography of their network, a complex sculpture, hoping to find a factual weakness, a chink in their armor. The minister's rebranding had hidden a darker purpose, a literal implosion of truth. Kael, feeling his destiny closing in, had to gamble. He purchased tickets to the terminus, ready to enter the core, a lancet of hope against their greedy tuition-funded empire. The misc collection of data would finally be undressed.

He lived roadside near the murky ocean terminus, his only hobbies being gardening a small juniper bush and reading ancient books. This tactical mission was temporary, a mere tangent from his boring everyday life, but driven by a deep vengeance. He knew the pickpockets and rowdy guards were just a trick, not the true obstacle. rawPanelSentences: Kael, a scruffy hacker and underdog, had a vast backlog of cases. He lived roadside near the murky ocean terminus, his only hobbies being gardening a small juniper bush and reading ancient books. He understood the urbane minister's palatial business was built on lossless data theft, granting them immunity. This was a constant warning, a modern fable of power. His nerves were always on edge, knowing his meticulous preparations for entry needed to discover the core theory of their black hole operations, despite the hardship.

This tactical mission was temporary, a mere tangent from his boring everyday life, but driven by a deep vengeance. He knew the pickpockets and rowdy guards were just a trick, not the true obstacle. The whole operation was under lockdown. His destiny was clear: to inject a trojan, overclocking their systems past their capacity. He needed to be fast, like an acrobat doing a backflip, avoiding radars and saccade movements. A specialist lockkeeper secured the station, while a veejay kept the guards distracted with tonal frequencies and pictures of nymphets. He had no time for mojitos.

The occidental security system was omnipotent, protected by a deejay's alarms, its tectonic plates shifting with every entry attempt. Kael knew it was a guessing game, requiring humility, not temper. He used knifes for precise cuts, avoiding detection. The skyjack and hijacker, part of a diversion, drew attention while he slipped past, handgun holstered. He witnessed a deputy licking a bone, a bizarre sight. Then, the grim outcome: he saw pictures of his family, worshipped by kings, their faces amber from nutrient deficiency, a stark testimony to the cruelty. This wasn't just business; it was personal.

With chagrin, he realized his underwear was showing as he performed a quick backflip, narrowly escaping detection. This was a wholly unexpected ramification. He observed the grand regional geography of their network, a complex sculpture, hoping to find a factual weakness, a chink in their armor. The minister's rebranding had hidden a darker purpose, a literal implosion of truth. Kael, feeling his destiny closing in, had to gamble. He purchased tickets to the terminus, ready to enter the core, a lancet of hope against their greedy tuition-funded empire. The misc collection of data would finally be undressed.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a lean figure with glowing green cybernetic eyes, wearing a dark hooded cloak and fingerless data-gloves. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Kael, a scruffy hacker, crouches roadside, looking at a towering, ornate corporate headquarters in the distance, a murky ocean behind him. Camera Angle: Wide shot, low angle. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Kael, mid-air doing a backflip, dodging a laser grid, his face determined as he slips past a heavily armored lockkeeper. Camera Angle: Dynamic, eye-level shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Kael, hidden in shadows, peeking into a control room, sees a deputy figure licking a bone while projected images of his family flash across a large screen, the images glowing amber. Camera Angle: Close-up, slightly from behind Kael's shoulder. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Kael, now at the heart of the facility, stands before a grand, complex network sculpture, holding a glowing data lancet, ready to plunge it into a pulsating core. Camera Angle: Medium shot, slight Dutch angle, cinematic lighting, bold ink outlines.

definitions.example: ambered wine, an ambered room

definitions.definition: To preserve in amber.
definitions.example: an ambered fly

definitions.definition: To cause to take on the yellow colour of amber.
To take on the yellow colour of amber.
Of a brownish yellow colour, like that of most amber.
The end or final point of something.
The end point of a transportation system, or the town or city in which it is located.
A boundary or border, or a post or stone marking such a boundary.
A person who is trained to preach, to perform religious ceremonies, and to afford pastoral care at a Protestant church.
definitions.example: The minister said a prayer on behalf of the entire congregation.

definitions.definition: A politician who heads a ministry (national or regional government department for public service).
definitions.example: He was newly appointed to be Minister of the Interior.

definitions.definition: At a diplomacy, the rank of diplomat directly below ambassador.
A servant; a subordinate; an officer or assistant of inferior rank; hence, an agent, an instrument.
To attend to (the needs of); to tend; to take care (of); to give aid; to give service.
To function as a clergyman or as the officiant in church worship
To afford, to give, to supply.
A Cuban cocktail, generally made from rum, lime, sugar, mint, etc.
A Western Christian of the Latin rite
Of, pertaining to, or situated in, the occident, or west; western.
definitions.example: occidental climates, or customs; an occidental planet.

definitions.definition: Of a gem or precious stone: of inferior value or quality.
A specific commercial enterprise or establishment.
definitions.example: I was left my father's business.

definitions.definition: A person's occupation, work, or trade.
definitions.example: He is in the motor and insurance businesses.

definitions.definition: Commercial, industrial, or professional activity.
definitions.example: He's such a poor cook, I can't believe he's still in business!

definitions.definition: The volume or amount of commercial trade.
definitions.example: Business has been slow lately.

definitions.definition: One's dealings; patronage.
definitions.example: I shall take my business elsewhere.

definitions.definition: Private commercial interests taken collectively.
definitions.example: This proposal will satisfy both business and labor.

definitions.definition: The management of commercial enterprises, or the study of such management.
definitions.example: I studied business at Harvard.

definitions.definition: A particular situation or activity.
definitions.example: This UFO stuff is a mighty strange business.

definitions.definition: Any activity or objective needing to be dealt with; especially, one of a financial or legal matter.
definitions.example: Let's get down to business.

definitions.definition: Something involving one personally.
definitions.example: That's none of your business.

definitions.definition: (parliamentary procedure) Matters that come before a body for deliberation or action.
definitions.example: If that concludes the announcements, we'll move on to new business.

definitions.definition: (travel) Business class, the class of seating provided by airlines between first class and coach.
Action carried out with a prop or piece of clothing, usually away from the focus of the scene.
The collective noun for a group of ferrets.
Something very good; top quality. (possibly from "the bee's knees")
definitions.example: These new phones are the business!

definitions.definition: Excrement, particularly that of a non-human animal.
definitions.example: As the cart went by, its horse lifted its tail and did its business.

definitions.definition: Of, to, pertaining to or utilized for purposes of conducting trade, commerce, governance, advocacy or other professional purposes.
definitions.example: Please do not use this phone for personal calls; it is a business phone.

definitions.definition: Professional, businesslike, having concern for good business practice.
Supporting business, conducive to the conduct of business.
Recognition of importance or value; respect; veneration (of someone, usually for being morally upright or successful)
definitions.example: The crowds gave the returning general much honor and praise.

definitions.definition: The state of being morally upright, honest, noble, virtuous, and magnanimous; excellence of character; the perception of such a state; favourable reputation; dignity
definitions.example: He was a most perfect knight, for he had great honor and chivalry.

definitions.definition: A token of praise or respect; something that represents praiseworthiness or respect, such as a prize or award given by the state to a citizen
definitions.example: Audie Murphy received many honors, such as the Distinguished Service Cross.

definitions.definition: A privilege
definitions.example: I had the honour of dining with the ambassador.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) the privilege of going first
definitions.example: I'll let you have the honours, Bob—go ahead.

definitions.definition: A cause of respect and fame; a glory; an excellency; an ornament.
definitions.example: He is an honour to his nation.

definitions.definition: (feudal law) a seigniory or lordship held of the king, on which other lordships and manors depended
The center point of the upper half of an armorial escutcheon (compare honour point)
In bridge, an ace, king, queen, jack, or ten especially of the trump suit. In some other games, an ace, king, queen or jack.
(in the plural) (courses for) an honours degree: a university qualification of the highest rank
definitions.example: At university I took honours in modern history.

definitions.definition: To think of highly, to respect highly; to show respect for; to recognise the importance or spiritual value of
definitions.example: The freedom fighters will be forever remembered and honored by the people.

definitions.definition: To conform to, abide by, act in accordance with (an agreement, treaty, promise, request, or the like)
definitions.example: I trusted you, but you have not honored your promise.

definitions.definition: To confer (bestow) an honour or privilege upon (someone)
definitions.example: Ten members of the profession were honored at the ceremony.

definitions.definition: To make payment in respect of (a cheque, banker's draft, etc.)
definitions.example: I'm sorry Sir, but the bank did not honour your cheque.

definitions.definition: Fearless; bold; brave.
To push or pump (something, especially fluids) into a cavity or passage.
definitions.example: The nurse injected a painkilling drug into the veins of my forearm.

definitions.definition: To introduce (something) suddenly or violently.
definitions.example: Punk injected a much-needed sense of urgency into the British music scene.

definitions.definition: To administer an injection to (someone or something), especially of medicine or drugs.
definitions.example: Now lie back while we inject you with the anesthetic.

definitions.definition: To take or be administered something by means of injection, especially medicine or drugs.
definitions.example: It's been a week since I stopped injecting, and I'm still in withdrawal.

definitions.definition: To introduce (code) into an existing program or its memory space, often without tight integration and sometimes through a security vulnerability.
To cast or throw; used with on.
An almost transparent aquatic animal; any one of the acalephs, especially one of the larger species, having a jellylike appearance.
Someone who listens, especially to a speech or a broadcast.
(chiefly Java) A function that runs in response to an event; an event handler.
A person's ear.
The person assigned to look after a canal or river lock, operating it and organizing its maintenance.
A train that calls at only some stations it passes between its origin and destination, typically just the principal stations
Firmly or securely fixed in place; stable.
definitions.example: That rope is dangerously loose. Make it fast!

definitions.definition: Firm against attack; fortified by nature or art; impregnable; strong.
(of people) Steadfast, with unwavering feeling. (Now mostly in set phrases like fast friend(s).)
Moving with great speed, or capable of doing so; swift, rapid.
definitions.example: I am going to buy a fast car.

definitions.definition: Causing unusual rapidity of play or action.
definitions.example: a fast billiard table

definitions.definition: (of a piece of hardware) Able to transfer data in a short period of time.
Deep or sound (of sleep); fast asleep (of people).
(of dyes or colours) Not running or fading when subjected to detrimental conditions such as wetness or intense light; permanent.
definitions.example: All the washing has come out pink. That red tee-shirt was not fast.

definitions.definition: Tenacious; retentive.
Having an extravagant lifestyle or immoral habits.
definitions.example: a fast woman

definitions.definition: Ahead of the correct time or schedule.
definitions.example: There must be something wrong with the hall clock. It is always fast.

definitions.definition: (of photographic film) More sensitive to light than average.
In a firm or secure manner, securely; in such a way as not to be moved; safe, sound .
definitions.example: Hold this rope as fast as you can.

definitions.definition: (of sleeping) Deeply or soundly .
definitions.example: He is fast asleep.

definitions.definition: Immediately following in place or time; close, very near .
definitions.example: Fast by the sturdy batsman the ball unheeded sped. / That ain't my style, said Casey. Strike one, the umpire said.

definitions.definition: Quickly, with great speed; within a short time .
definitions.example: Do it as fast as you can.

definitions.definition: Ahead of the correct time or schedule.
definitions.example: I think my watch is running fast.

definitions.definition: Short for "stand fast", a warning not to pass between the arrow and the target
A fictitious narrative intended to enforce some useful truth or precept, usually with animals, etc. as characters; an apologue. Prototypically, Aesop's Fables.
Any story told to excite wonder; common talk; the theme of talk.
Fiction; untruth; falsehood.
The plot, story, or connected series of events forming the subject of an epic or dramatic poem.
To compose fables; hence, to write or speak fiction; to write or utter what is not true.
To make up; to devise, and speak of, as true or real; to tell of falsely; to recount in the form of a fable.
An athlete who performs acts requiring skill, agility and coordination.
Malware that appears to perform or actually performs a desired task for a user while performing a harmful task without the user's knowledge or consent.
To infect (a system) with a trojan.
Describing a satellite (moon or minor planet) that shares an orbit with another
A tendency to be in a certain type of mood; a habitual way of thinking, behaving or reacting.
definitions.example: to have a good, bad, or calm temper

definitions.definition: State of mind; mood.
A tendency to become angry.
definitions.example: He has quite a temper when dealing with salespeople.

definitions.definition: Anger; a fit of anger.
definitions.example: an outburst of temper

definitions.definition: Calmness of mind; moderation; equanimity; composure.
definitions.example: to keep one's temper; to lose one's temper; to recover one's temper

definitions.definition: Constitution of body; the mixture or relative proportion of the four humours: blood, choler, phlegm, and melancholy.
Middle state or course; mean; medium.
The state of any compound substance which results from the mixture of various ingredients; due mixture of different qualities.
definitions.example: the temper of mortar

definitions.definition: The heat treatment to which a metal or other material has been subjected; a material that has undergone a particular heat treatment.
The state of a metal or other substance, especially as to its hardness, produced by some process of heating or cooling.
definitions.example: the temper of iron or steel

definitions.definition: (sugar manufacture) Milk of lime, or other substance, employed in the process formerly used to clarify sugar.
To moderate or control.
definitions.example: Temper your language around children.

definitions.definition: To strengthen or toughen a material, especially metal, by heat treatment; anneal.
definitions.example: Tempering is a heat treatment technique applied to metals, alloys, and glass to achieve greater toughness by increasing the strength of materials and/or ductility. Tempering is performed by a controlled reheating of the work piece to a temperature below its lower eutectic critical temperature.

definitions.definition: To sauté spices in ghee or oil to release essential oils for flavouring a dish in South Asian cuisine.
To mix clay, plaster or mortar with water to obtain the proper consistency.
To adjust, as the mathematical scale to the actual scale, or to that in actual use.
(Latinism) To govern; to manage.
To combine in due proportions; to constitute; to compose.
To mingle in due proportion; to prepare by combining; to modify, as by adding some new element; to qualify, as by an ingredient; hence, to soften; to mollify; to assuage.
To fit together; to adjust; to accommodate.
Consisting of a variety of ingredients or parts.
Having diverse characteristics, abilities or appearances.
Hard to see through, as a fog or mist.
Dark, dim, gloomy.
Cloudy, indistinct, obscure.
Dishonest, shady.
That which is produced or occurs as a result of an event or process.
definitions.example: A quality automobile is the outcome of the work of skilled engineers and thousands of workers.

definitions.definition: The result of a random trial. An element of a sample space.
definitions.example: Three is a possible outcome of tossing a six-sided die.

definitions.definition: The anticipated or desired results or evidence of a learning experience (often used in the phrase learning outcomes).
definitions.example: The outcomes of this course are outlined in your syllabus.

definitions.definition: The scoreline; the result.
One appointed as the substitute of another, and empowered to act for them, in their name or their behalf; a substitute in office
definitions.example: As the deputy store manager, he is able to fire staff.

definitions.definition: A person employed to install and remove props, brattices, etc. and to clear gas, for the safety of the miners.
(France): A member of the Chamber of Deputies, formerly called Corps Législatif
(Ireland): a member of Dáil Éireann, or the title of a member of Dáil Éireann. (Normally capitalised in both cases)
definitions.example: At today's meeting, Deputy Ryan will speak on local issues.

definitions.definition: (United States): a law enforcement officer who works for the county sheriff's office; a deputy sheriff or sheriff's deputy; the entry level rank in such an agency
definitions.example: The sheriff's deputies took the suspect into custody.

definitions.definition: To deputise
The "Enter" key on a computer keyboard.
A stroke of the Enter key.
To go or come into an enclosed or partially enclosed space.
definitions.example: You should knock before you enter, unless you want to see me naked.

definitions.definition: To cause to go (into), or to be received (into); to put in; to insert; to cause to be admitted.
definitions.example: to enter a knife into a piece of wood;  to enter a boy at college, a horse for a race, etc.

definitions.definition: To go or come into (a state or profession).
definitions.example: My twelve-year-old son will be entering his teens next year.  She had planned to enter the legal profession.

definitions.definition: To type (something) into a computer; to input.
definitions.example: Enter your user name and password.

definitions.definition: To record (something) in an account, ledger, etc.
To become a party to an agreement, treaty, etc.
To become effective; to come into effect.
To go into or upon, as lands, and take actual possession of them.
To place in regular form before the court, usually in writing; to put upon record in proper from and order.
definitions.example: to enter a writ, appearance, rule, or judgment

definitions.definition: To make report of (a vessel or its cargo) at the custom house; to submit a statement of (imported goods), with the original invoices, to the proper customs officer for estimating the duties. See entry.
To file, or register with the land office, the required particulars concerning (a quantity of public land) in order to entitle a person to a right of preemption.
To deposit for copyright the title or description of (a book, picture, map, etc.).
definitions.example: entered according to act of Congress

definitions.definition: To initiate; to introduce favourably.
An instance of hijacking; the illegal seizure of a vehicle; a hijacking.
An instance of a seizure and redirection of a process.
An amendment which deletes the contents of a bill and inserts entirely new provisions.
Preflop, the position two before the dealer.
To forcibly stop and seize control of some vehicle in order to rob it or to reach a destination (especially an airplane, truck or a boat).
To seize control of some process or resource to achieve a purpose other than its originally intended one.
To seize control of a networked computer by means of infecting it with a worm or other malware, thereby turning it into a zombie.
To change software settings without a user's knowledge so as to force that user to visit a certain web site (to hijack a browser).
To introduce an amendment deleting the contents of a bill and inserting entirely new provisions.
Someone who hijacks.
definitions.example: the hijacker of an aeroplane

definitions.definition: Hijackware.
Of or relating to a palace.
On a grand scale; with very rich furnishings.
definitions.example: The home where he lived was palatial.

definitions.definition: The inrush of air in forming a suction stop.
The action of imploding.
The act or action of bringing to or as if to a center.

Any considerable and connected part of a space or surface; specifically, a tract of land or sea of considerable but indefinite extent; a country; a district; in a broad sense, a place without special reference to location or extent but viewed as an entity for geographical, social or cultural reasons.
definitions.example: the equatorial regions

definitions.definition: An administrative subdivision of a city, a territory, a country.
The inhabitants of a region or district of a country.
A place in or a part of the body in any way indicated.
definitions.example: the abdominal regions

definitions.definition: Place; rank; station; dignity.
The space from the earth's surface out to the orbit of the moon: properly called the elemental region.
Any shrub or tree of the genus Juniperus of the cypress family, which is characterized by pointed, needle-like leaves and aromatic berry-like cones.
One of a number of coniferous trees which resemble junipers.
Gin.
A sharp, pointed, two-edged surgical instrument used in venesection and for opening abscesses etc.
A small, sterile single-use needle used to draw a drop of blood for testing, as with a glucometer.
An iron bar used for tapping a melting furnace.
A high narrow window, terminating in an arch acutely pointed, often double or triple, common in the first half of the 13th century.
To pierce with a lancet.
Someone who presents a television programme of videos; a video jockey.
To work as a video jockey; to present videos.
Having greed; consumed by selfish desires.
Prone to overeat.
Tending to match as much text as possible.
definitions.example: This regular expression performs a greedy match.

definitions.definition: (of an algorithm) That tries to find the global optimum by finding the local optimum at each stage.
(plural "grand") A thousand of some unit of currency, such as dollars or pounds. (Compare G.)
(plural "grands") A grand piano
Of a large size or extent; great.
definitions.example: a grand army

definitions.definition: Great in size, and fine or imposing in appearance or impression; illustrious, dignified, magnificent.
definitions.example: His simple vision has transformed into something far more grand.

definitions.definition: Having higher rank or more dignity, size, or importance than other persons or things of the same name.
definitions.example: The Grand Viziers of the Ottoman Empire.

definitions.definition: (usually in compound forms) Standing in the second or some more remote degree of parentage or descent.
definitions.example: grandfather, grandson, grand-child

definitions.definition: Fine; lovely.
definitions.example: A cup of tea? That'd be grand.

definitions.definition: Containing all the parts proper to a given form of composition.
To grow plants in a garden; to create or maintain a garden.
definitions.example: I love to garden — this year I'm going to plant some daffodils.

definitions.definition: Of a batsman, to inspect and tap the pitch lightly with the bat so as to smooth out small rough patches and irregularities.
The process or action of cultivating the soil, particularly in a garden; the care of a garden; horticulture.
definitions.example: His hobby was gardening.

definitions.definition: The act of a batsman prodding at the pitch with his bat, typically in order to flatten any bumps
A summons to arms, as on the approach of an enemy.
Any sound or information intended to give notice of approaching danger; a warning sound to arouse attention; a warning of danger.
A sudden attack; disturbance.
Sudden surprise with fear or terror excited by apprehension of danger; in the military use, commonly, sudden apprehension of being attacked by surprise.
A mechanical device for awaking people, or rousing their attention.
definitions.example: The clock radio is a friendlier version of the cold alarm by the bedside.

definitions.definition: An instance of an alarm ringing, beeping or clanging, to give a noise signal at a certain time.
definitions.example: You should set the alarm on your watch to go off at seven o'clock.

definitions.definition: To call to arms for defense
To give (someone) notice of approaching danger
To rouse to vigilance and action; to put on the alert.
To surprise with apprehension of danger; to fill with anxiety in regard to threatening evil; to excite with sudden fear.
To keep in excitement; to disturb.
A sum of money paid for instruction (such as in a high school, boarding school, university, or college).
definitions.example: These rosemaling workshops are no place for anyone who wants to pester me or the students with the "white privilege" card, inter alia. Therefore, I reserve the right to refund the tuition of such men and women, kick them out the door, and bar them from at least two of my future events.

definitions.definition: The training or instruction provided by a teacher or tutor.
Care, guardianship.
A description of an event or system that is considered to be accurate.
Mental conception; reflection, consideration.

A coherent statement or set of ideas that explains observed facts or phenomena and correctly predicts new facts or phenomena not previously observed, or which sets out the laws and principles of something known or observed; a hypothesis confirmed by observation, experiment etc.
The underlying principles or methods of a given technical skill, art etc., as opposed to its practice.
A field of study attempting to exhaustively describe a particular class of constructs.
definitions.example: Knot theory classifies the mappings of a circle into 3-space.

definitions.definition: A hypothesis or conjecture.

A set of axioms together with all statements derivable from them; or, a set of statements which are deductively closed. Equivalently, a formal language plus a set of axioms (from which can then be derived theorems). The statements may be required to all be bound (i.e., to have no free variables).
definitions.example: A theory is consistent if it has a model.

definitions.definition: An omnipotent being.
Having unlimited power, force or authority.
Describing a cell (especially a stem cell) that is capable of developing into any type of cell or forming any type of tissue (also called a totipotent cell). See also pluripotent.
A disc jockey.
To perform as a disc jockey.
A male monarch; a man who heads a monarchy. If it's an absolute monarchy, then he is the supreme ruler of his nation.
definitions.example: Henry VIII was the king of England from 1509 to 1547.

definitions.definition: A powerful or majorly influential person.
definitions.example: Howard Stern styled himself as the "king of all media".

definitions.definition: Something that has a preeminent position.
definitions.example: In times of financial panic, cash is king.

definitions.definition: A component of certain games.
A king skin.
definitions.example: Oi mate, have you got kings?

definitions.definition: A male dragonfly; a drake.
A king-sized bed.
A male person.
To crown king, to make (a person) king.
To rule over as king.
To perform the duties of a king.
To assume or pretend preeminence (over); to lord it over.
To promote a piece of draughts/checkers that has traversed the board to the opposite side, that piece subsequently being permitted to move backwards as well as forwards.
To dress and perform as a drag king.
A drinking game that uses playing cards.
A sounding stone, a Chinese musical instrument.
A collection of sheets of paper bound together to hinge at one edge, containing printed or written material, pictures, etc.
definitions.example: He was frustrated because he couldn't find anything about dinosaurs in the book.

definitions.definition: A long work fit for publication, typically prose, such as a novel or textbook, and typically published as such a bound collection of sheets, but now sometimes electronically as an e-book.
definitions.example: I have three copies of his first book.

definitions.definition: A major division of a long work.
definitions.example: Genesis is the first book of the Bible.

definitions.definition: A record of betting (from the use of a notebook to record what each person has bet).
definitions.example: I'm running a book on who is going to win the race.

definitions.definition: A convenient collection, in a form resembling a book, of small paper items for individual use.
definitions.example: a book of raffle tickets

definitions.definition: The script of a musical or opera.
(usually in the plural) Records of the accounts of a business.
A book award, a recognition for receiving the highest grade in a class (traditionally an actual book, but recently more likely a letter or certificate acknowledging the achievement).
(whist) Six tricks taken by one side.
Four of a kind
A document, held by the referee, of the incidents happened in the game.
(by extension) A list of all players who have been booked (received a warning) in a game.
The twenty-sixth Lenormand card.
Any source of instruction.
An activity that one enjoys doing in one's spare time.
definitions.example: I like to collect stamps from different countries as a hobby.

definitions.definition: An extinct breed of horse native to the British Isles, also known as the Irish Hobby
Any of four species of small falcons in the genus Falco, especially Falco subbuteo.
The characteristic of being humble; humbleness in character and behavior.
A representation of anything (as a person, a landscape, a building) upon canvas, paper, or other surface, by drawing, painting, printing, photography, etc.
An image; a representation as in the imagination.
A painting.
definitions.example: There was a picture hanging above the fireplace.

definitions.definition: A photograph.
definitions.example: I took a picture of the church.

definitions.definition: A motion picture.
definitions.example: Casablanca is my all-time favorite picture.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) ("the pictures") Cinema (as a form of entertainment).
definitions.example: Let's go to the pictures.

definitions.definition: A paragon, a perfect example or specimen (of a category).
definitions.example: She's the very picture of health.

definitions.definition: An attractive sight.
definitions.example: The garden is a real picture at this time of year.

definitions.definition: The art of painting; representation by painting.
A figure; a model.
Situation.
definitions.example: The employment picture for the older middle class is not so good.

definitions.definition: To represent in or with a picture.
To imagine or envision.
To depict or describe vividly.
Cinema, movie theatre
definitions.example: We go to the pictures every Saturday night.

definitions.definition: An artificial intelligence researcher who believes that intelligence is too complicated (or computationally intractable) to be solved with the sorts of homogeneous system favoured by the "neats".
Untidy in appearance.
definitions.example: A scruffy little dog came scampering down the road.

definitions.definition: Scurfy.
A three dimensional work of art created by shaping malleable objects and letting them harden or by chipping away pieces from a rock (sculpting).
Works of art created by sculpting, as a group.
The three-dimensional ornamentation on the outer surface of a shell
To fashion something into a three-dimensional figure.
To represent something in sculpture.
To change the shape of a land feature by erosion etc.
A bundle of neurons with their connective tissue sheaths, blood vessels and lymphatics.
definitions.example: The nerves can be seen through the skin.

definitions.definition: A neuron.
A vein in a leaf; a grain in wood
definitions.example: Some plants have ornamental value because of their contrasting nerves.

definitions.definition: Courage, boldness.
definitions.example: He hasn't the nerve to tell her he likes her. What a wimp!

definitions.definition: Patience.
definitions.example: The web-team found git-sed is really a time and nerve saver when doing mass changes on your repositories

definitions.definition: Stamina, endurance, fortitude.
Audacity, gall.
definitions.example: He had the nerve to enter my house uninvited.

definitions.definition: (polymer technology) The elastic resistance of raw rubber or other polymers to permanent deformation during processing.
(in the plural) Agitation caused by fear, stress or other negative emotion.
definitions.example: Ellie had a bad case of nerves before the big test.

definitions.definition: Sinew, tendon.
To give courage.
definitions.example: May their example nerve us to face the enemy.

definitions.definition: To give strength; to supply energy or vigour.
definitions.example: The liquor nerved up several of the men after their icy march.

definitions.definition: A source of nourishment, such as food, that can be metabolized by an organism to give energy and build tissue.
Providing nourishment.
